title: Backtest of my Nasdaq Algo
author: Some_Fly_4552
selftext: Hello guys, here you can see a pretty decent backtest of my nasdaq algo. It is suitable for prop firms like FTMO, Fundednext, or The5ers. You can try it out, there’s a free trial of 30 days. Just dm me to get more info✌️

comments.author: BezisThings
comments.body: If it comes with a subscription its full of shit

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Just look at the chart bro, why would I?

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: And there’s a free trial with a one time payment not a subscription

comments.author: ionone777
comments.body: 15 % risk per trade ? ain't a little much

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: You can change it to how much you want to we recommend 1.5% risk per trade it is suitable for line Ftmo, Fundednext, The5ers

comments.author: Lopsided-Rate-6235
comments.body: huge draw downs  based on your test. Also its a grid strategy so you need to improve risk management alot. your top monthly PNL was 21k before it went negative with one loss. thats a bad strategy

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: Yeah because of such high risk, for normal prop firms it is 1.5% risk and in our group we always devise people to turn it off on this day because of too high volatility so this is not 100% accurate.

comments.author: No_Pollution9224
comments.body: What exactly is special about that non-predictive grid strategy?

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: It is not special you can do it yourself if you’d like to. But still takes time and patience to write the code.

comments.author: Fun-Reception-6897
comments.body: OP, you know very well that if your algo worked, you wouldn't be selling it. you'd be using it yourself to make bank.

replies.author: Some_Fly_4552
replies.body: I still make bank, but why should I not share it? As you can see it works on the 4 year backtesting?
